title: Cirrus: Task consolidation
body:
The set of Cirrus-CI tasks seems to be ever-expanding, in addition to a tangled dependency-tree.  This is slowing down the overall CI process slightly (due to task queuing) and adds to the mental load required to service the YAML.  Fix both by consolidating several semi-related tasks into a slightly larger build-task.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Woopsie this isn't ready yet....still need to do something with the validate and consistency checks.
This is ready for review.
@lsm5 @edsantiago PTAL

New task map: 
![cirrus-map-pr15782](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/191494896-4fc75cc6-9ea8-469b-8227-307e640491fc.png)
This is way too complicated for me to review in the next half-hour, sorry. It'll have to be after f2f.

> This is way too complicated for me to review in the next half-hour, sorry. It'll have to be after f2f.

It's not a problem.  It's an increase in YAML & script complexity in in favor of questionably faster CI.  I wanted to roll the `Validate` task (and a few others) up into `Build`, but that was even more complex :disappointed:   Perhaps this can be done in a future PR.

> Whew. Okay, I think I'm done. LGTM with a few suggestions, make of them what you will. Thanks for the cleanup!

Thanks for your scrutiny, and @lsm5 too!
/lgtm
/hold
Sorry Dan, this is not ready; it's pending fixes.
Sorry about that,
@cevich quick reminder, this is in your ballpark

> this is in your ballpark

Yep, I haven't forgotten.  Just letting it marinate a bit :grin:
Force-push: Applied all suggestions, rebased on main.
force-push: mid-air collision, rebased and resolved conflicts.
force-push: Squashed fixup commit.
Force-push: Added shellcheck skip for Ubuntu.
Force-push: Shellcheck fixups.
Force-push: Rebased & resolved conflicts.
LGTM but the `SC2154` bug is triggering. (As in, bug in the linter, which is not set up to understand our shared code base)

> LGTM but the SC2154 bug is triggering

I haven't found a good way around that.  There's no easy way to get shellcheck to account for use of the shared automation library or variables set by Cirrus-CI.  The check can be outright disabled, but then we miss out on finding actually undefined variables.  I don't think there's a good solution, so it's easier to just double-check and disable it as findings pop up.
Oof, maybe I should just disable it.  We do have and use the `req_env_vars` function anyway, so the SC2154 thing is less useful in this repo.

> We do have and use the req_env_vars function anyway

That doesn't help against typos:
```bash
req_env_vars SCRIPT_BASE
... $SCRITP_BASE
```
...but then, neither does shellcheck.

/lgtm
/hold

I reviewed using `range-diff`, not the thorough very-long review I did on my first pass; I'm puzzled by putting `check_go_changes` in `postbuild` (it seems to make more sense in `prebuild`, at least to me) but that's not important enough to block on.

> I'm puzzled by putting check_go_changes in postbuild

FWIW, my decision was based on the (possibly tenuous) argument: It *Compiles*, is slightly more important than, it *looks good*.  Assuming current and future `check_go_changes` checks are all functionally equivalent.

>It Compiles, is slightly more important than, it looks good

Except: "it compiles" is not useful when the very next step is "sorry bubba, you have to fix and re-push", so it doesn't really matter if it compiles :wink: . But still, not important enough to block for.

/hold cancel
